subject: [OOM-3] Docker credentials secret missing
commit_message:
[OOM-3] Docker credentials secret missing


Change-Id: I30df3cc11315b757a1d3e3da832681682a970825
Signed-off-by: Borislav Glozman <Borislav.Glozman@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Based on OOM-3 the issue is really not being able to DL docker images because credentials aren't there. I believe the image pull policy change and the addition of prepare-image.sh are fine, but not in the scope of this BUG. Hence I'd rather have those two changes track by two others Jira, with the fix in seperated patches.
Thanks for the contribution.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Can you explain why you opted to use the imagePullPolicy of IfNotPresent instead of Always?  With IfNotPresent, any updates to images in the registry will not be automatically deployed when the pods restart.  It adds an extra layer of management for someone to now ssh to EACH k8s worker node and run the image pull script.  I don't think this is the right way of doing this..the easiest way is to just let kubernetes pull the images that are newer than what is currently deployed and this happens automatically when you set the pullPolicy to Always.  i believe the default policy is also Always.

The other comment about these changes is that we did not have to use docker secrets to have k8s be able to pull from ONAP's registry.  We simply configured our docker daemons to properly use insecure-registries and went from there.  There were no issues pulling images etc when the k8s environment is setup to handle that.  Did you consider that before going ahead with adding additional logic for the docker secrets etc?

Patch Set 1:

> (5 comments)
 > 
 > Can you explain why you opted to use the imagePullPolicy of
 > IfNotPresent instead of Always?  With IfNotPresent, any updates to
 > images in the registry will not be automatically deployed when the
 > pods restart.  It adds an extra layer of management for someone to
 > now ssh to EACH k8s worker node and run the image pull script.  I
 > don't think this is the right way of doing this..the easiest way is
 > to just let kubernetes pull the images that are newer than what is
 > currently deployed and this happens automatically when you set the
 > pullPolicy to Always.  i believe the default policy is also Always.
 > 
 > The other comment about these changes is that we did not have to
 > use docker secrets to have k8s be able to pull from ONAP's
 > registry.  We simply configured our docker daemons to properly use
 > insecure-registries and went from there.  There were no issues
 > pulling images etc when the k8s environment is setup to handle
 > that.  Did you consider that before going ahead with adding
 > additional logic for the docker secrets etc?

Hi Mandeep,
Thank you for your comments! I will try to address them.
We changed to use imagePullPolicy because it takes 40-60 minutes in our 
lab to pull all the images (on each node), preventing ONAP successful deployment. 
Current ONAP deployment design requires all components to start in less than 10 
minutes...
Default imagePullPolicy is IfNotPresent 
according to: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/ .
We added however it in all places as 
1. Declarative - easier for human to read, 
2. In order to be able to change it easily in script to "Never" for making sure all
images are pulled in advance.
Regarding pulling latest version of images during pods restart - it may be
dangerous in a production environment - I would prefer more control, using versioning.

We are striving to make the onap deployment k8s infrastructure agnostic, 
meaning it will run not only on Rancher. On Rancher after adding "Registry" the secrets 
are indeed not needed. However on our k8s, without Rancher (and I assume 
that it will be like this for most deployments) secrets are required. Insecure-registries 
are not something I would like to make customers to configure unless it is completely 
necessary - it is a workaround that has to be done on all k8s dones. 

Hope my logic is convincing.
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.
Patch Set 3: Published edit on patch set 2.
Patch Set 4: Published edit on patch set 3.
Patch Set 5: Patch Set 4 was rebased
Change has been successfully merged by Yury Novitsky

Patch Set 5:

I am good with docker secret credentials addition.
But reviewing the path for volumes, it is using /dockerdata-nfs which is very environment specific. At some point that would have to be driven by parameters
